_comment: Sample seed data for local Strapi development. Import via Strapi admin UI or a custom seed script.

interest-clusters.label: Tech & Code
interest-clusters.description: Software engineering, programming languages, tools, and technology culture.

interest-clusters.label: Books & Ideas
interest-clusters.description: Literature, non-fiction, philosophy, and the ideas that shape how we see the world.

projects.title: konnichiwa. — Personal Website
projects.description: A multi-tier personal site built with Astro, React, ASP.NET Core, and Strapi. My corner of the internet.
projects.body:
## Overview

This site is built as a full-stack monorepo: public Astro site, React Admin PWA, .NET 10 API, and Strapi CMS — all hosted on Azure.

projects.title: Reading Tracker
projects.description: A small CLI tool to track books, articles, and papers. Syncs to a local SQLite database.

projects.title: Open Source Contributions
projects.description: Ongoing contributions to open source projects across frontend tooling and developer experience.

readings.title: A Philosophy of Software Design
readings.author: John Ousterhout
readings.note: One of the most concise, practical takes on software complexity I've read.

readings.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
readings.author: David Thomas & Andrew Hunt
readings.note: Timeless advice. Some examples are dated but the principles hold up.

readings.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
readings.author: Daniel Kahneman
readings.note: Changed how I think about my own decision-making. Subscriber-only reflection in the note.

milestones.title: Started High School
milestones.description: Enrolled in a science-track secondary school — the beginning of many late-night study sessions and lifelong friendships.

milestones.title: First Open Source Contribution
milestones.description: Merged my first pull request into an open source project — a tiny docs fix that felt enormous at the time.

milestones.title: University — Computer Science
milestones.description: Began a BSc in Computer Science. Chose software engineering as my specialisation after falling in love with system design.

milestones.title: First Engineering Internship
milestones.description: Spent a summer building internal tooling at a tech company. Shipped my first feature to production and survived code review.

milestones.title: Built Something for Fun
milestones.description: Stayed up all night building a small reading tracker app just because I wanted to. No deadline, no requirements — pure joy.

milestones.title: Graduated
milestones.description: Walked across the stage with a BSc in Computer Science. Four years of coffee, compilers, and good people.

milestones.title: Something Big (TBD)
milestones.description: Whatever comes next — still writing this chapter.

family-members.name: Mum
family-members.tagline: The person who somehow always knows when something is wrong before I do.
funFacts: Makes the best mint tea in the world — no contest
Has read more books than anyone I know
Can navigate any city without a map

family-members.name: Dad
family-members.tagline: Engineer by trade, storyteller by nature — where I get both the logic and the drama.
funFacts: Built our first family computer from scratch
Has a story for every occasion

family-members.name: Little Sibling
family-members.tagline: Funnier than me, braver than me, and growing up way too fast.
funFacts: Can recite entire movie scripts from memory
Beat me at chess at age nine
Has excellent taste in music (admitted reluctantly)

education.description: Focus on software engineering and distributed systems.

experience.description: Building scalable web services and internal tooling.
